description: How to build websites that speak multiple languages — exploring i18n strategies, locale switching, and the UX considerations that make global audiences feel at home.
content:
Building for a Global Audience

[!CAUTION] The Complexity of i18n The hardest part about internationalization (i18n) isn't necessarily the translation itself, but ensuring the UI accommodates fluctuating string lengths, right-to-left languages (RTL), and different cultural dates or currency formats.

[!TIP] CSS Logical Properties Instead of using margin-left or padding-right, switch over to CSS Logical Properties like margin-inline-start or padding-inline-end. This guarantees your layout will effortlessly flip when reading Arabic or Hebrew without writing an entirely new stylesheet!

description: A deep dive into collecting, analysing, and acting on user feedback online — from ratings and reviews to heatmaps and session recordings.
content:
Automating User Insights

[!CAUTION] The Noise Problem When you open the floodgates to online feedback, the sheer volume of noise can drown out the signal. You end up reading thousands of identical bug reports or generic complaints.

[!TIP] The Solution: NLP Filtering We implemented a lightweight NLP classifier over our incoming feedback ingest stream. It automatically categorizes feedback into Feature Request, Bug Report, and UX Complaint, and routes them to the correct PM's backlog.

description: A practical guide to integrating third-party features — APIs, auth, analytics, payments — into modern web apps without sacrificing performance or maintainability.
content:
Handling Dependencies Like a Pro

[!CAUTION] Integration Hell Dropping a shiny new feature into an existing monolithic codebase often breaks three other unrelated things. Identifying the blast radius of a new integration is terrifying in a large repo.

[!TIP] Feature Flags & Strangler Fig Utilize feature flags to conditionally roll out the new integration. Pair this with the Strangler Fig pattern where you slowly route traffic to the new microservice instead of doing a risky "Big Bang" release.
